body_txt: Major Characters. Leads. Principals. Main Characters. Central Characters—they're whom the story is about. There's generally one lead character, The Protagonist . Sometimes, there's also a Co-Protagonist, who shares the central role with another main character, and Deuteragonist , the second important character. On rare occasions, there's a "Tritagonist", but it doesn't go any further than that; if a fourth or even more lead characters should show up, then we're firmly in Ensemble Cast territory. So there are no "protagonists," just those three and other major characters. These other characters' main character status tends to be defined by how close they're to the lead(s). Basically, a main character is any character who has a major purpose or role in the plot and/or interacts regularly with the main characters. In many series, a name indicates that this character will be a main character , if only for one episode. Without these people, the series wouldn't be the same, and sometimes, it'd be entirely different. If the person has an effect on the plot but isn't affecting it by their merit, this is a MacGuffin . If the story is from first person, then the person speaking will almost always be a main character, though there are exceptions. If the story isn't from first person, then the characters you see around the most will generally be the main characters, though, once again, there are exceptions. See also:
